public_mentions.title: GSoC Spy: Track contributors in open-source projects | Jigyasu Rajput posted on the topic | LinkedIn
public_mentions.text:
GSoC Spy: Track contributors in open-source projects | Jigyasu Rajput posted on the topic | LinkedIn

Jigyasu Rajput

11mo

Want to check GSoC competition in a Huge Repo? Introducing GSoC Spy... Ever struggled to track active contributors in open-source projects? . Whether you're a GSoC mentor, organization admin, or a student aiming for GSoC, keeping up with contribution activity can be a challenge. That’s why I built GSoC Spy – an open-source web application that analyzes GitHub repositories to track pull request activity, helping identify top contributors over different time periods. Link ~ https://lnkd.in/gQbADt_e What Can GSoC Spy Do? 🔹 Analyze any GitHub repository – Fetch and filter PRs over 2 weeks, 1 month, 3 months, 6 months, or all time. 🔹 Get contributor insights – Distinguish

targeted_qa.projects:
Jigishaa Suresh is currently working as a Product Development Engineer at AMD, where her work involves hardware design and verification, particularly in areas such as RTL design, FPGA development, and computer architecture concepts like pipelining and caching ([search results](#result-1)). While her profile highlights her skills and coursework related to ASIC design, FPGA implementation, and digital logic, it does not specify any particular notable projects or products she has built. 

Based on the available information, her contributions seem to be centered around hardware development and validation in a professional setting rather than publicly documented projects or products. She has worked on projects such as the design of a low-cost mini stereo digital audio processor and hardware IP implementation on Zybo Zynq-7000 boards ([search results](#result-1)). However, these are more individual or academic projects rather than widely recognized products or groundbreaking innovations. 

Therefore, as of now, there are no specific notable technical projects or products publicly attributed to Jigishaa Suresh beyond her professional work at AMD and her academic projects.

According to her professional profile, she is proficient in Python, SQL, data analysis, data visualization, machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing (NLP), and computer vision ([Data Science Portfolio](https://www.datascienceportfol.io/JigishaP)). Additionally, her background indicates a focus on crafting intelligent solutions using these technologies, which are critical in modern AI-driven applications.
